title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Eye Twitching
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great way to relieve eye twitching. Some herbs, like Valeriana officinalis, have a calming effect on the nerves, which can help to stop the twitching. The active compounds in these herbs, such as valerenic acid, work to slow down the nervous system and promote relaxation.  Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender oil, has a similar effect. It contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension. When applied topically or inhaled, lavender oil can help to soothe the eye muscles and stop the twitching.  Another herb, Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm oil, can also be helpful. It contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This can help to reduce eye irritation and inflammation, which can contribute to eye twitching.  Drinking herbal teas made from these herbs can also provide relief from eye twitching. The active compounds in the herbs can be absorbed through the digestive system and enter the bloodstream, where they can help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension.  Using herbal essential oils for eye twitching can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can provide quick and natural relief from eye twitching, which can be annoying and distracting. It can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can have a positive impact on your overall well-being.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for eye twitching

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovaleric acid, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds help to calm the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and stress that can cause eye twitching. The sedative and anxiolytic properties of Valeriana officinalis essential oils also help to soothe the nervous system, making it an effective natural remedy for eye twitching. The antispasmodic properties of valerenic acid help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching. By relaxing the nervous system and reducing muscle tension, Valeriana officinalis essential oils can provide relief from eye twitching.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Valeriana officinalis roots and 1 cup of carrier oil like coconut oil or jojoba oil.
Wash the roots with water and pat them dry. Use a sharp knife to chop the roots into small pieces.
Fill a clean glass jar with 2 cups of chopped roots and add 1 cup of carrier oil. Stir well.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the roots to infuse their oil into the carrier oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and use the essential oil as needed for eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have a calming and relaxing effect on the nervous system. Linalool, in particular, has a sedative property that can help reduce muscle tension and spasms that may be contributing to eye twitching. The antispasmodic properties of linalyl acetate can also help to calm the muscles around the eyes, reducing twitching and spasms. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may be beneficial in alleviating eye twitching.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat. Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the water.
Let the mixture simmer for 1-2 hours. This will help release the oils from the flowers.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Discard the flowers.
Let the mixture sit for 24 hours to allow the oil to separate from the water. Use a dropper to collect the oil and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains linalool, limonene, and citral as its active constituents. These compounds have a relaxing and calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce eye twitching caused by stress and anxiety. Linalool, in particular, has a sedative property that can help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms. The antispasmodic properties of limonene and citral can also help to relax the muscles around the eyes and reduce twitching. By using Melissa officinalis essential oils, you can potentially alleviate eye twitching and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan. Heat the water over low heat.
Let the mixture simmer for 30 minutes, then turn off the heat. Let it sit for 2 hours.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of a carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil) to the liquid and mix well.
Bottle the mixture and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops of the oil in the eye area 2-3 times a day for eye twitching relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils contains flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching. The essential oil also contains phytosterols, such as beta-sitosterol, which have been shown to have a soothing effect on the nervous system and can help to calm muscle spasms. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata essential oil has been found to have a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of eye twitching. By using Passiflora incarnata essential oils, you can potentially alleviate eye twitching by addressing the underlying causes of this condition.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil).
Combine the flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a small saucepan and heat it gently over low heat (around 100°F to 110°F).
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture steep for 2-3 hours. Strain and bottle the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and ease spasms in the muscles around the eyes, often causing eye twitching. The essential oil's analgesic and sedative properties can also calm the nervous system, reducing irritation and stress that may trigger eye twitching. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health. By using Zingiber officinale essential oils, you may experience relief from eye twitching due to its soothing and calming effects on the muscles and nervous system.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger roots and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and add the ginger roots.
Simmer for 30 minutes to release ginger's oils and flavor.
Let the mixture cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Use a dropper to extract the essential oil from the ginger mixture and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils contains saponins, avenacosides, and other bioactive constituents that work together to help alleviate eye twitching. Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. Avenacosides, on the other hand, have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, helping to calm eye muscles and reduce spasms. The sedative properties of Avena sativa essential oils also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of eye twitching. By combining these properties, Avena sativa essential oils may help to provide relief from eye twitching and promote overall eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa seeds and 4 cups of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Heat 2 cups of carrier oil in a double boiler or a saucepan with a heat-proof bowl.
Add 1 cup of Avena sativa seeds to the heated oil and mix well. Let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the seeds.
Bottle the Avena sativa essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops as needed to help with eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce spasms, which can contribute to eye twitching. The terpenoids, such as bilobalide, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm and relax the muscles around the eyes. By reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and calming the nervous system, Ginkgo biloba essential oils may help to alleviate eye twitching.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Heat a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil.
Let the mixture steep for 2-3 hours, then strain the leaves from the oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the oil to a clean glass bottle and add 10 drops of vitamin E oil to preserve the essential oil. Store in a cool, dark place.
To use, apply 2-3 drops of the Ginkgo biloba essential oil to the affected eye area 2-3 times a day. Massage gently.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains a compound called alkylamides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, which can cause twitching. The essential oil also contains sesquiterpene lactones, such as echinacoside, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These compounds work together to calm and soothe the eye muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of twitches. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Echinacea purpurea essential oils can help to alleviate eye twitching.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine 1/2 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1/2 cup of carrier oil in the glass jar. Close the lid and shake well.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day. This step helps the oil extract from the flowers.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the extracted oil to a small bottle and label it. Use 2-3 drops of the oil in a carrier oil for eye massage to help with eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains menthone and menthol as its active constituents. These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety. Menthol also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated tissues and reduce spasms. The essential oil's vasodilating properties can increase blood flow to the eyes, reducing tension and promoting relaxation. By applying Mentha x piperita essential oils topically or inhaling its vapors, you may experience relief from eye twitching due to its soothing and calming effects.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita (peppermint) leaves, 1 cup of water, and a glass jar with a lid.
Wash the peppermint leaves and chop them into small pieces. Add them to the glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of water over the peppermint leaves in the jar. Close the lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.

Use a dropper to transfer the peppermint essential oil to a small glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dark place and use 2-3 drops in a carrier oil for massage or apply it to the affected area to help relieve eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula latifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce tension and stress, which are common causes of eye twitching. The sedative and anxiolytic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of these compounds can help relieve muscle tension and cramps in the face and eyes. By calming the nervous system and reducing muscle tension, Lavandula latifolia essential oils may help alleviate eye twitching and provide relief from this condition.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula latifolia herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Lavandula latifolia leaves and 4 cups of water in a pot.
Heat the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a large bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the liquid cool and separate into a cup of essential oil and a cup of water. Use the essential oil.
Use 2-3 drops of the Lavandula latifolia essential oil in a carrier oil or diffuser for relief from eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, including silymarin and silibinin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce swelling and oxidative stress in the body. By using Silybum marianum essential oils, you can benefit from its ability to calm and soothe the nervous system, which can contribute to eye twitching. The anti-inflammatory properties of silymarin and silibinin can also help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the eyes, which may be a contributing factor to twitching. Regular use of Silybum marianum essential oils may help to alleviate eye twitching by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for eye twitching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum seeds, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Fill the glass jar with 1 cup of Silybum marianum seeds and cover it with a lid.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil to the jar and close it. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the seeds.
Store the Silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Use 2-3 drops of oil under your tongue or on your skin for relief from eye twitching.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent eye twitching. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and lavender, contain calming properties that can relax muscles and soothe the nervous system. This can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of eye twitching. Drinking a warm cup of herbal tea before bed may also promote a good night's sleep, helping to prevent twitching from occurring.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option to help with eye twitching, but consuming them daily isn't always safe. Some herbs, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered safe, but others, like sage and rosemary, can cause issues if taken excessively. It's best to try a small amount and see how you react.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with eye twitching, but it's hard to say exactly how long it takes to see results. Some people report feeling relief within a few days, while others may need a week or two to notice a difference. It's different for everyone, and it may take some trial and error to find what works best.
faq_time: If you experience eye twitching, try drinking herbal tea in the evening, right before bed. The calming effects of the tea can help relax your muscles and reduce twitching. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and lavender, are known to promote relaxation and can help you unwind after a long day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications in unexpected ways. If you're taking medication for eye twitching, be cautious with teas that contain caffeine or certain herbs like valerian root or chamomile. These can enhance or cancel out the effects of your medication, so it's essential to understand the potential interactions before consumption.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and experiencing eye twitching, herbal teas might seem like a soothing solution. However, some herbs can be too strong for your body, especially your baby's. For example, peppermint and sage can cause uterine contractions. It's best to stick with gentle teas like chamomile or raspberry leaf, which are often recommended for pregnant women.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, some herbal teas can be safe to try for eye twitching. However, it's essential to know the ingredients. Peppermint and chamomile teas are often used to calm nerves and soothe the body. But be careful with teas that contain caffeine or other stimulants, as they can affect milk production. Choose a tea that's caffeine-free and gentle.
title_2: Sleep Deprived and Experiencing Eye Twitching? Try 11 Essential Oils
